frameworks.name: Hebbian / activity-dependent plasticity
frameworks.one_line: tDCS-induced M1 plasticity is gated by concurrent neural engagement.

frameworks.name: top-down attentional gain
frameworks.one_line: Selective attention modulates the magnitude of stimulation-induced cortical change.

core_question: Does engaging selective attention during anodal tDCS over M1 enhance stimulation-induced cortical excitability and motor learning?
key_assumptions: anodal tDCS over M1 can induce motor cortical plasticity
motor cortical plasticity underlies motor learning
manipulations: attention-paired vs passive tDCS during stimulation
key_findings: attention-paired tDCS produced larger MEP increases than passive tDCS
attention-paired tDCS yielded faster motor learning on the serial reaction-time task
interpretations: top-down attention gates the tDCS plasticity effect on M1
cognitive engagement is a determinant of stimulation-induced behavioral gain
